<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <utility>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(targetName);
    
    // Get the singleton ObserverGraph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // Notify the target - this should trigger the observer
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify observer was notified (call count should be 1)
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Record call count before reset
    int initialCallCount = observer->getCallCount();
    
    // Reset the observer node
    observer->reset();
    
    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != initialCallCount) {
        return false;
    }
    
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

bool test_node_closure(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>("target");
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverClosure>(nodeName);

    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();

    // Add observer relationship: target observes the observer
    graph.addRelationship(target, observer);

    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->valueChanged(true);
    if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    // Reset observer state
    observer->valueChanged(false);

    // Close observer node
    observer->close();

    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);

    // Verify observer was not notified after closure
    if (observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false;
    }

    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

    return capturedValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

int test_reactive_update_chain(int a, int b, int newA) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    
    // Create first calculation (depends on var_a and var_b)
    auto calc1 = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        return var_a.get() + var_b.get(); 
    });
    
    // Create second calculation (depends on calc1)
    auto calc2 = reaction::calc([&]() { 
        return calc1.get() * 2; 
    });
    
    // Update the first variable
    var_a.set(newA);
    
    // Return the final result of the chain
    return calc2.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age) 
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.set(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.set(age); }

    // Public variables for reactive calculations
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    PersonRecord person(initialName, initialAge);
    
    // Create a reactive calculation that depends on both name and age
    auto info = reaction::calc([](const std::string& name, int age) {
        return "Name: " + name + ", Age: " + std::to_string(age);
    }, person.m_name, person.m_age);
    
    // Update the fields reactively
    person.setName(newName);
    person.setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return the updated information
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,

                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    auto recompute_count = reaction::var(0);
    
    auto calc = reaction::calc([a, b, &recompute_count]() {
        return a.get() + b.get();

    b.set(nonBatchB);

    a.set(batchA);
    b.set(batchB);
    batch.execute();
    int after_batch = recompute_count.get();
    
    return {calc.get(), after_non_batch, after_batch};
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create named reactive variables
    auto varA = reaction::Var<int>("varA", valueA);
    auto varB = reaction::Var<int>("varB", valueB);
    
    // Create named calculation
    auto calc_sum = reaction::Calc<int>("calc_sum", [&]() {
        return varA.get() + varB.get();
    });
    
    // Create named action
    auto action_mult = reaction::Action("action_mult", [&]() {
        int result = varA.get() * varB.get();
        // Action execution logic (result can be used as needed)
    });
    
    // Retrieve and combine names
    std::string combined_names = varA.getName() + "," + 
                                varB.getName() + "," + 
                                calc_sum.getName() + "," + 
                                action_mult.getName();
    
    return combined_names;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

The function should:

2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
3. Create filter-triggered calculation
4. Apply filter

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

    double lastValue = 0.0;
    
    // Apply change filter to the sensor
    auto filteredSensor = sensor.filter(reaction::filter::ChangeFilter(diffThreshold));
    
    // Create calculation that depends on filtered sensor
    auto statusCalc = reaction::calc([&, filteredSensor]() {
        double current = filteredSensor.get();
        recomputeCount++;
        lastValue = current;
        return current > levelThreshold ? "HIGH" : "NORMAL";
    });
    
    // Update sensor with first value
    sensor.set(firstUpdate);
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount;
    
    // Update sensor with second value  
    sensor.set(secondUpdate);
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount;
    
    // Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusCalc.get();
    
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    tm.reset();
    tm.registerThread();
    
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyBefore = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    std::thread workerThread([&tm]() {
        tm.registerThread();
    });
    
    workerThread.join();
    
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = tm.getThreadCount();
    bool safetyAfter = tm.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        threadCountBefore,
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&recomputeCount](int value) {
        recomputeCount++;
        return value;
    }, reactiveVar);
    
    int beforeBatch = recomputeCount;
    
    reaction::batch_start();
    reactiveVar += deltaA;
    reactiveVar += deltaB;
    reaction::batch_end();
    
    int afterBatch = recomputeCount;
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{beforeBatch, afterBatch, finalValue};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchLifecycleResult {
    bool closedInitially;
    bool closedAfterClose;
    int recomputeCountAfterExecute;

BatchLifecycleResult test_batch_manual_lifecycle(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Step 1: Create reactive variable
    reaction::Var<int> reactVar(initialValue);
    
    // Step 2: Track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;

    }).depends_on(reactVar).set_name("test_calc");

    auto observer = calc.observe([&](const auto& result) {
        recomputeCount++;
    });
    
    // Step 4: Create batch
    reaction::Batch batch;
    
    // Step 5: Check if batch is closed initially
    bool closedInitially = batch.is_closed();
    
    // Step 6: Execute batch
    batch.execute();
    
    // Step 7: Record recompute count after execute

    // Add operation to update reactive variable in batch
    batch.add([&]() {
        reactVar.set(updatedValue);
    });
    
    // Step 8: Close batch
    batch.close();

    bool closedAfterClose = batch.is_closed();
    
    // Step 10: Ensure calculation is evaluated (recomputeCount should be 1)

        closedInitially,

    };

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

ThreadSafetyTransition test_thread_manager_manual() {
    auto& threadManager = reaction::concurrency::ThreadManager::get_instance();
    threadManager.reset();
    threadManager.register_current_thread();
    std::size_t threadCount = threadManager.get_thread_count();
    bool beforeEnable = threadManager.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    threadManager.enable_thread_safety();
    bool afterEnable = threadManager.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    threadManager.reset();
    bool afterReset = threadManager.is_thread_safety_enabled();
    
    return ThreadSafetyTransition{
        beforeEnable,
        afterEnable,
        afterReset,
        threadCount
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;
};

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {
    auto var = reaction::var(initialValue);
    auto counter = reaction::var(0);
    
    auto calc = reaction::calc([var, counter]() {
        int doubled = var.get() * 2;
        counter.set(counter.get() + 1);
        return doubled;
    }, reaction::AlwaysTrig{});
    
    auto result = reaction::var(calc);
    
    int recomputeCountAfterInit = counter.get();
    
    var.set(initialValue);
    
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite = counter.get();
    
    // Force evaluation to avoid unused variable warnings
    int dummy = result.get();
    
    return {recomputeCountAfterInit, recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {

    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;

    // Create a reactive variable
    auto var = reaction::Var(initialValue);

    auto calc = reaction::Calc([&var]() { return var.get() * 2; }, reaction::LastHandle{});

    int initialResult = calc.get();
    
    // Update the source variable
    var.set(updatedValue);
    
    // Get updated result
    int updatedResult = calc.get();
    
    // Close the source node
    var.close();
    
    // Get result after source close
    int afterCloseResult = calc.get();
    
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = afterCloseResult
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    StackMonitor::disable();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Create report struct
    StackMonitorReport report;
    
    // Step 4: Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = StackMonitor::is_enabled();
    
    // Step 5: Create StackGuard in a scope block
    bool guardCreated = false;
    try {
        StackGuard guard;
        guardCreated = true;
        
        // Step 6: Check if guard was successfully allocated
        report.guardAllocated = guardCreated;
        
        // Step 7: Get current usage during guard
        report.usageDuringGuard = StackMonitor::get_current_usage();
        
        // Step 8: Get statistics
        auto stats = StackMonitor::get_stats();
        report.usagePercentage = stats.usage_percentage;
        report.nearLimitDuringGuard = stats.near_limit;
        
        // Guard goes out of scope here
    } catch (...) {
        // Handle allocation failure
        report.guardAllocated = false;
    }
    
    // Step 11: Get current usage after guard
    report.usageAfterGuard = StackMonitor::get_current_usage();
    
    // Step 12: Disable monitoring again
    StackMonitor::disable();
    
    // Step 13: Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = StackMonitor::is_enabled();
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <array>
#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/sbo_resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct SmallPayload {
    int value;
    bool operator!=(const SmallPayload& other) const = default;
};

struct LargePayload {
    std::array<int, 64> data{};
    bool operator!=(const LargePayload& other) const = default;
};

struct SboStorageStatus {
    bool updateReportedChange;
    bool smallUsingSBO;
    std::size_t smallStackUsage;
    bool largeInitialized;
    bool largeUsingSBO;
};

SboStorageStatus test_sbo_storage_info(int initialSmall,
                                       int updatedSmall,
                                       int largeSeed) {
    // Lambda to create large payload with sequential values based on seed
    auto makeLargePayload = [](int seed) -> LargePayload {
        LargePayload payload;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < payload.data.size(); ++i) {
            payload.data[i] = seed + static_cast<int>(i);
        }
        return payload;
    };

    // Create small SBOResource with initial value
    reaction::SBOResource<SmallPayload> smallResource(SmallPayload{initialSmall});
    
    // Create large SBOResource with payload generated by lambda
    reaction::SBOResource<LargePayload> largeResource(makeLargePayload(largeSeed));
    
    // Update small resource and check if change was reported
    bool updateReportedChange = smallResource.updateValue(SmallPayload{updatedSmall});
    
    // Retrieve storage information
    auto smallInfo = smallResource.getStorageInfo();
    auto largeInfo = largeResource.getStorageInfo();
    
    // Construct and return the status
    SboStorageStatus status{
        .updateReportedChange = updateReportedChange,
        .smallUsingSBO = smallInfo.usingSBO,
        .smallStackUsage = smallInfo.stackUsage,
        .largeInitialized = largeInfo.initialized,
        .largeUsingSBO = largeInfo.usingSBO
    };
    
    return status;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourcePointerSnapshot {
    int initialPointerValue;
    int pointerValueAfterWrite;
    bool pointerStable;
};

ResourcePointerSnapshot test_resource_pointer_view(int initialValue, int directWriteValue) {
    // Create a Resource instance with the initial value
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    
    // Get the first raw pointer and read its value
    int* ptr1 = resource.getPtr();
    int initialPointerValue = *ptr1;
    
    // Write directly through the pointer
    *ptr1 = directWriteValue;
    
    // Get the second raw pointer and read the updated value
    int* ptr2 = resource.getPtr();
    int pointerValueAfterWrite = *ptr2;
    
    // Check if the pointer remains stable (same address)
    bool pointerStable = (ptr1 == ptr2);
    
    return ResourcePointerSnapshot{
        .initialPointerValue = initialPointerValue,
        .pointerValueAfterWrite = pointerValueAfterWrite,
        .pointerStable = pointerStable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    auto source = React<int>::create(initialValue);
    auto copy = source;
    
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    source.close();
    
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);

    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables with the provided names
    auto var1 = React::var(firstName);
    auto var2 = React::var(secondName);
    auto var3 = React::var(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into a set to automatically order using React::operator<
    std::set<React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names from the set
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    for (const auto& react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.name());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique by comparing set size to 3
    bool allUnique = (reactSet.size() == 3);
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Check if the owner was notified before deletion. That's the first part of the result. Then clear the owner's notification count so we can test the post-deletion scenario.

Finally, close the nodes and return the result structure with the two boolean values.

Now, looking at the code structure. The `TrackingFieldNode` class needs to track notifications. The `valueChanged` method increments the counter. The `notifications()` method returns the count, and `clear()` resets it.

In the implementation, I need to use the reaction library's functions. For example, `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance().registerNode(owner);` and similar for the field node. Then for the field graph, `reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance().registerFieldNode(objectId, field);`.

Binding would be something like `reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance().bindField(objectId, field, owner);`. Triggering a change on the field would call `field->valueChanged(true);`, which should notify the owner.

After clearing dependencies and mappings, trying to bind again might fail, but the code should attempt it anyway. Then another change and check.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // 1. Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    TrackingFieldNode owner("OwnerNode");
    TrackingFieldNode field("FieldNode");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.registerNode(&field);
    
    // 4. Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Register field nodes under object ID
    fieldGraph.registerFieldNode(objectId, &field);
    
    // 6. Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on field
    field.valueChanged(true);
    
    // 8. Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = (owner.notifications() > 0);
    
    // 9. Clear owner notification count
    owner.clear();
    
    // 10. Clear dependencies on owner
    fieldGraph.clearDependencies(&owner);
    
    // 11. Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.removeFieldMappings(objectId);
    
    // 12. Attempt to bind field again
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    
    // 13. Trigger notification on field again
    field.valueChanged(true);
    
    // 14. Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = (owner.notifications() > 0);
    
    // 15. Close nodes
    observerGraph.closeNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.closeNode(&field);
    
    // 16. Return field graph cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    StackLimitSnapshot snapshot;
    
    // 1. Enable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().enable();
    
    // 2. Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().initialize();
    
    // 3. Get usage statistics before guard
    snapshot.statsBeforeGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().getUsageStats();
    
    // 4. Check if template allocation is allowed
    const size_t testSize = 1024; // Example allocation size
    snapshot.templateAllocationAllowed = 
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocateTemplate(testSize);
    
    // 5. Check if runtime allocation is allowed  
    snapshot.runtimeAllocationAllowed = 
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().canAllocateRuntime(testSize);
    
    // 6. Create large StackGuard instance
    auto guard = reaction::memory::StackGuard(testSize * 10); // Large guard size
    
    // 7. Check if guard succeeded
    snapshot.guardHeld = guard.success();
    
    // 8. Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    bool allocationBlocked = false;
    try {
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().allocateRuntime(1);
    } catch (const std::runtime_error&) {
        allocationBlocked = true;
    }
    snapshot.allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlocked;
    
    // 9. Get usage statistics after guard
    snapshot.statsAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getInstance().getUsageStats();
    
    return snapshot;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

void CountingObserver::valueChanged(bool changed) {
    m_notifications++;
}

int CountingObserver::notifications() const {
    return m_notifications;
}

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto ob = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<ObserverNode>();

    auto& graph = ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    graph.registerNode(ob);
    graph.registerNode(source1);
    graph.registerNode(source2);
    graph.registerNode(source3);
    
    graph.attachObserver(source1, ob);
    source1->notify(true);
    
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial = ob->notifications();
    
    graph.detachObserver(source1, ob);
    graph.attachObserver(source2, ob);
    graph.attachObserver(source3, ob);
    
    source1->notify(true);
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate = ob->notifications();
    
    source2->notify(true);
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate = ob->notifications();
    
    source3->notify(true);
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate = ob->notifications();
    
    graph.closeNode(ob);
    graph.closeNode(source1);
    graph.closeNode(source2);
    graph.closeNode(source3);
    
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstNotificationsAfterInitial,
        firstNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        secondNotificationsAfterUpdate,
        thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LogicalExpressionSnapshot {
    bool initialAnd;
    bool initialOr;
    bool updatedAnd;
    bool updatedOr;
};

inline LogicalExpressionSnapshot test_logical_expression_builders(bool a, bool b, bool c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a_var = reaction::var(a);
    auto b_var = reaction::var(b);
    auto c_var = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Build logical expressions using calc with lambdas
    auto and_expr = reaction::calc([](bool a_val, bool b_val) { 
        return a_val && b_val; 
    }, a_var, b_var);
    
    auto or_expr = reaction::calc([](bool a_val, bool b_val) { 
        return a_val || b_val; 
    }, a_var, b_var);
    
    // Get initial results
    bool initialAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool initialOr = or_expr.get();
    
    // Update variables to trigger recomputation
    a_var.set(!a);
    b_var.set(!b);
    
    // Get updated results
    bool updatedAnd = and_expr.get();
    bool updatedOr = or_expr.get();
    
    return LogicalExpressionSnapshot{
        .initialAnd = initialAnd,
        .initialOr = initialOr,
        .updatedAnd = updatedAnd,
        .updatedOr = updatedOr
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_thread_manager_force_register` function.

The function should:
1. Get thread manager instance
2. Reset thread manager state
3. Check thread safety before registration
4. Register main thread
5. Check thread safety after main thread registration
6. Create worker thread and register it
7. Wait for worker thread to complete
8. Check thread safety after worker thread registration

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *
 * Collects observable state around calls to ThreadManager registration APIs.
 */
struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {
    bool safetyBeforeForce;
    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;
};

/**
 * Function: test_thread_manager_force_register
 *
 * Demonstrates how registering multiple distinct threads causes
 * ThreadManager to enable thread safety.
 *
 * - We start from a clean ThreadManager state.
 * - The main thread registers itself and remains the sole thread.
 * - A worker thread then also registers, which should trigger
 *   multi-thread detection and enable thread safety.
 *
 * @return ForceRegisterSnapshot
 *         A snapshot of the thread-safety flag before any registration,
 *         after the main-thread registration, and after the worker-thread registration.
 */
inline ForceRegisterSnapshot test_thread_manager_force_register();

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```
